name: claudecog
description: A cognitive layer for code. ClaudeCog reads any local repo as a living system, maps its architecture in 3D, explains files like a senior engineer, and surfaces real risks.
long_description:
ClaudeCog turns chaotic codebases into something you can think on top of.

Point it at a folder on your machine and Claude will:

- map the system into modules and show them in an interactive 3D walkthrough
- explain any file like a thoughtful tech lead pairing with you
- surface the risks that will actually hurt the team in production or onboarding

This extension talks to your own Anthropic account using the API key you paste below. Nothing leaves your machine except the file summaries sent to Claude.

tools.name: cog_map
tools.description: Map a local repository as a system. Returns modules, relationships, summary, and a link to a 3D walkthrough.

tools.name: cog_explain
tools.description: Explain a single file like a senior engineer pairing with you for ten minutes.

tools.name: cog_risks
tools.description: Surface the real risks that will hurt this team in production, in 6 months, or during onboarding.

api_key.title: Anthropic API key
api_key.description: Your personal Anthropic API key. Get one at https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys

model.title: Claude model
model.description: Which Claude model ClaudeCog should use to think.

lang.title: Output language
lang.description: Language for ClaudeCog responses. Use 'en', 'pt' or 'es'.
